item.text:
New tankers have a double hull which gives |protection*protect| to the cargo.

Manifolds |connect*connection| deck pipelines with shore hoses or loading arms.

A crew member is standing at the manifolds, in the case of a leaking |connection*connect| or any other problem.

The main pipelines |connect*connection| with cargo pumps and the deck pipelines.

Tankers |carry*carriage| liquid bulk cargo in cargo tanks.

The master of a tanker is responsible for the safe and secure |mooring*moor| of the ship.

Before |pumping*pump| begins, all terminal and tanker valves in the transfer system are opened.

|Loading*Load| starts at a low rate to a single tank.

The crew member is then ready to instruct the shore to stop |loading*load|.

At the end of |loading*load| operation the cargo tanks are topped-up.

The |loading*load| is finished and all valves in the cargo system are closed.

Oil terminals are situated in locations where tankers can |load*loading| or discharge their cargo.
